cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the author's hope for his son was. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand it properly. The first paragraph says, "Every man wants his son to be somewhat of a clone, not in features but in footsteps." So the author is talking about wanting his son to follow in his footsteps, meaning to take up his ambitions or path, not to look like him physically. Then it mentions that as the father ages, his own ambitions become unachievable, so he hopes his son can accomplish
